text: The patient is positioned supine on the operating table, and trocars are placed for robotic-assisted surgery. Adhesiolysis is performed to identify the herniated root limb, with a lighted bougie inserted to aid in its delineation from surrounding adhesions. The gastrojejunal anastomosis is freed from mediastinal attachments, followed by a posteriocuroplasty.

The surgeon focuses on the left diaphragm, identifying the buccal lac hernia defect. The omentum and colon are reduced back into the abdomen. Sharp and blunt lysis of adhesions exposes the diaphragm musculature, followed by primary repair using non-absorbable sutures.
The surgeon places a composite mesh in an onlay fashion to reinforce the repaired area, followed by the application of a bioabsorbable mesh to further reinforce the hiatal hernia repair.
